entities.name: FOOD-BORNE DISEASES
entities.description: Diseases caused by consuming contaminated food, often resulting from poor hygiene and handling practices

entities.name: FOOD HYGIENE AND HANDLING PRACTICES
entities.description: The set of behaviors and procedures used to maintain food safety and cleanliness at home

entities.name: HYDERABAD
entities.description: The urban and peri-urban area in India where the study was conducted

entities.name: INDIA
entities.description: The country where the study took place

entities.name: REFRIGERATOR
entities.description: An appliance used for the cold storage of food to prevent spoilage

entities.name: TEMPERATURE
entities.description: The thermal state of the refrigerator used to assess food storage quality

entities.name: EDIBILITY
entities.description: The criteria used by residents, such as smell and appearance, to determine if food is fit for consumption

entities.name: NON-VEGETARIAN FOOD
entities.description: Animal-source foods requiring specific handling and storage indicators

entities.name: LATENT CLASS ANALYSIS
entities.description: A statistical technique used to identify unobserved subgroups within a population based on heterogeneity of traits

entities.name: LATENT CLASS MODELLING
entities.description: A method used to analyze the relationship between covariates and latent class membership

entities.name: SOCIO-DEMOGRAPHIC VARIABLES
entities.description: Characteristics such as education, age, and income

entities.name: SOCIO-ECONOMIC VARIABLES
entities.description: Factors related to social class and economic status

entities.name: SOCIO-SPATIAL VARIABLES
entities.description: Factors related to the geographic and social environment

entities.name: HEALTH OUTCOMES
entities.description: Physical health results used as covariates in the study

entities.name: DIETARY OUTCOMES
entities.description: Results related to food consumption and nutrition used as covariates

entities.name: FOOD SAFETY PRACTICES
entities.description: Broad interventions and behaviors aimed at reducing food-borne illness in developing countries

entities.name: OBESITY
entities.description: A health condition used as a covariate to distinguish latent classes in households without refrigerators
